Required States: Loading hero, empty featured, personalization unavailable
Key Components: Hero, search, category chips, featured products, trust strip
UX Notes: Make search dominant; keep CTAs simple: Shop now, Sell on Swyftly.

Required States: Skeleton grid, no products, filter loading
Key Components: Product grid, filter chips, sort, pagination/infinite scroll
UX Notes: Use sticky filter/sort controls on mobile.

Key Components: Category header, subcategory chips, products, filter drawer
UX Notes: Fashion categories need filters visible above the fold.

Entry Points: Search bar, AI assistant, category page
Key Components: Search bar, result count, filters, sort, product cards
UX Notes: Preserve filters in URL query params.

Key Components: Filter groups, chips, price slider, size/colour/brand facets
UX Notes: On mobile, filter drawer should show result count and sticky Apply button.

Required States: Out of stock, variant unavailable, seller unavailable
Key Components: Image gallery, variant picker, price, seller card, delivery/returns, reviews
UX Notes: Make size/variant selection mandatory before Add to cart.
Mobile gallery should be full-screen with swipe and thumb strip.

Key Components: Store hero, seller rating, products, policies
UX Notes: Show verified status and delivery/return policy summary.

Key Components: Wishlist product cards, remove, move to cart
UX Notes: Allow guest wishlist locally, prompt login to persist.
Helpful for product discovery but not MVP-critical.

Required States: Empty cart, item removed, price changed
Key Components: Drawer, mini cart items, subtotal, checkout CTA
UX Notes: Use non-blocking drawer after add-to-cart; preserve shopping context.

Required States: Empty cart, stock changed, single-seller restriction
Key Components: Cart list, quantity controls, seller grouping, totals
UX Notes: For MVP, clearly warn if cart is limited to one seller.

Required States: No address, invalid address, save failed
Key Components: Checkout stepper, address cards, address form
UX Notes: Guest checkout can be later; MVP can require login for safer orders.

Required States: No delivery option, seller shipping issue
Key Components: Shipping options, delivery estimate, policy note
UX Notes: Default to available shipping; show total updates immediately.

Key Components: Payment provider frame/redirect, summary, security reassurance
UX Notes: Do not collect raw card data directly unless PCI scope is handled.

Key Components: Order summary, address, shipping, items, policies
UX Notes: Optional; if payment page already includes summary, this can be merged.

Key Components: Progress state, reassurance copy, retry timer
UX Notes: Never leave user on blank spinner; explain what is happening.

Key Components: Success panel, order summary, next steps
UX Notes: Show clear delivery and seller fulfilment expectations.

Key Components: Failure message, retry CTA, support link
UX Notes: Use calm copy; keep cart reserved only if allowed by timer.
Mobile order cards should show status and next action first.

Required States: Shipment not available, return window closed
Key Components: Status timeline, items, seller, payment, delivery, actions
UX Notes: Use one contextual primary action based on order status.

Key Components: Tracking timeline, courier info, delivery address
UX Notes: Make delivery uncertainty visible, not hidden.

Key Components: Rating, text area, image upload optional
UX Notes: Ask separately for product and seller fulfilment experience.

Required States: Return window closed, item not eligible
Key Components: Eligibility summary, product selector, policy copy
UX Notes: Use plain language; avoid legal-heavy text.

Key Components: Reason picker, notes, photo upload, refund expectation
UX Notes: Show required evidence only for relevant reasons.

Key Components: Return timeline, seller/admin responses, refund estimate
UX Notes: Timelines reduce support tickets.
Keep dispute messages structured; avoid free-form chaos.
Keep minimum data; collect only useful profile info.
Reusable address component for checkout and account.
May be unnecessary if using hosted checkout only.
Notifications should deep-link to the object they mention.
Support should start from object context: order/product/seller.
Reduce support load with clear returns, payout, fees info.
AI must only recommend real products from backend results.
Group recommendations by outfit, occasion, budget.

Entry Points: Search bar camera icon, AI assistant
UX Notes: Make image upload frictionless; show examples.
Let users edit detected category/colour/style.
After login, return user to original intent.
Keep buyer registration short.
Avoid revealing whether email exists.
Clear next step based on role.
Emphasise free listings and only pay when you sell.
Show the full process so sellers know what is needed.
Auto-generate slug; preview as seller types.
Support both individual and business sellers.
Sensitive changes later require additional verification.
This address feeds fulfilment and return workflows.
Use clear seller obligations: product accuracy, shipping, returns.

Key Components: Pending card, checklist, expected next steps
UX Notes: Let seller draft products while verification is pending, but not publish/sell.
Provide specific next actions, not vague rejection.

Key Components: KPI cards, tasks, recent orders, AI CTA
UX Notes: Use dashboard as action hub, not just metrics.
Preview should mirror buyer-facing storefront.

Key Components: Product table/cards, status filters, bulk actions
UX Notes: Mobile should use product cards instead of dense table.

Key Components: Image uploader, drag/drop, reorder, photo tips
UX Notes: Start with images because fashion is visual; allow skip with warning.

Key Components: Title, short notes, description, brand, condition
UX Notes: Show AI CTA after minimum notes/images are present.
Attributes must change by category.

Key Components: Variant matrix, size/colour rows, SKU, stock
UX Notes: Use bulk add sizes/colours for clothing.

Key Components: Price, sale price, fee preview, seller earnings
UX Notes: Show estimated payout to build trust.

Key Components: Shipping options, handling time, return policy
UX Notes: Default from seller store settings but allow override.
Preview should reveal exactly what buyer will see.
Set expectation for admin/moderation state.

Key Components: Status timeline, product data, moderation notes
UX Notes: Always show why product is not published yet.

Key Components: AI prompt input, context summary, generate CTA
UX Notes: Frame AI as helper: “Generate draft” not “auto-publish”.
Allow user to continue with text-only suggestions if image analysis fails.

Required States: Invalid JSON handled server-side, low confidence category
Key Components: Suggestion cards: title, description, category, attrs, tags
UX Notes: Allow field-by-field acceptance, not all-or-nothing.
Clicking each missing field should jump to the exact form control.

Key Components: Risk cards, explanation, safe wording suggestions
UX Notes: Use neutral language: “needs confirmation” not accusation.

Key Components: CSV uploader, column mapping, validation results
UX Notes: Useful later for larger boutiques.
Surface low stock and reserved stock separately.
Require reason for audit trail.
Status tabs: New, Processing, Shipped, Returns.

Key Components: Order summary, buyer shipping details, items, payout hold
UX Notes: Show payout status linked to order status.
Reduce mistakes with item checklist and variant details.

Key Components: Courier selector, tracking number, dispatch date
UX Notes: Buyer notification fires after save.

Key Components: Return reason, evidence, policy, response options
UX Notes: Show response deadline clearly.
Explain payout impact before seller confirms.
Link tickets to order/product when possible.

Key Components: Balance cards, pending/available/on hold, ledger list
UX Notes: Use plain language for pending vs available funds.
Include payment provider reference when available.

Key Components: Bank details, payout schedule, security warning
UX Notes: Require extra auth/verification for changes.

Key Components: Sales KPIs, conversion, traffic, top products
UX Notes: Launch once tracking data is meaningful.
Connect analytics to action: improve or promote.
Show ad eligibility before asking seller to configure campaign.

Key Components: Campaign type cards: featured product, sponsored search, store spotlight
UX Notes: Start simple with featured products before CPC complexity.

Key Components: Product selector, quality score, stock status
UX Notes: Disable ineligible products with reason.
Keep targeting simple for MVP ads.
Forecast must be labelled estimated.
Show ad preview and total max spend clearly.

Key Components: Status header, budget spend, product list
UX Notes: Do not allow editing fields that break active campaign billing.
Explain attribution window clearly.
Changes to public store should preview before save.
Separate urgent operational items from general updates.

Key Components: Seller FAQs, fees, shipping, payout guide
UX Notes: Make policies discoverable before disputes happen.
Admin access should be separate and protected.

Key Components: KPI cards, action queues, risk alerts
UX Notes: Prioritise work queues over vanity metrics.

Key Components: Queue table, risk filters, SLA badges
UX Notes: Sort by risk/age of request.

Key Components: Seller data, documents, storefront preview, notes
UX Notes: Every decision needs a reason and audit log.

Key Components: Risk signals, disputes, refunds, flags, actions
UX Notes: Do not automate punitive actions without human review initially.
Show high-risk products first.

Key Components: Product preview, seller, AI flags, notes
UX Notes: Admin sees buyer preview plus hidden risk data.

Key Components: Flag details, phrases, image refs, AI summary
UX Notes: Allow marking false positives to improve rules.

Key Components: Reason templates, custom note, policy reference
UX Notes: Use templates for consistency and seller clarity.
Category changes affect product forms and filters.
This powers dynamic product creation forms.
Luxury brands can trigger proof requirements.
Support needs fast lookup by email/order.
Limit sensitive data by role.

Key Components: Seller details, products, orders, payouts, flags
UX Notes: One central seller view reduces admin context switching.

Key Components: Order table, status filters, payment state
UX Notes: Search by order ID, buyer, seller, tracking.

Key Components: Order timeline, buyer/seller, items, payment, shipment, return
UX Notes: Admin order detail is the operational source of truth.
Webhook idempotency visibility is important.
Show debits/credits and related order/payment/payout.
Sort by pending payment impact and age.
Use SLA badges to avoid stale disputes.
Decision should generate buyer/seller notifications.

Key Components: Payout table, seller risk, amount, status
UX Notes: High-risk payouts should be visually separated.

Key Components: Payout summary, related ledger, seller risk, actions
UX Notes: Actions require confirmation and audit reason.
Restrict to finance admins only.
Use assignment and statuses early.
Internal notes must be visually distinct from public replies.
Ad approval protects buyer experience.

Key Components: Campaign summary, preview, product quality, seller risk
UX Notes: Ads must be clearly labelled and eligible.
Avoid exposing analytics before tracking is reliable.
Finance exports need date filters and audit trail.

Key Components: AI calls, cost estimate, latency, failures
UX Notes: Needed once AI usage grows.
Every sensitive action must be logged.
Later convert hard-coded rules into configurable rules.
Useful before large-scale operations.
Keep superadmin-only.

Key Components: Health cards, failed jobs, webhook logs
UX Notes: Critical for payments and background jobs.
Use layout-preserving skeletons instead of spinners where possible.
Every empty state should answer: why empty, what next?
Avoid generic “Something went wrong” only.
Explain role needed without exposing sensitive info.
Useful once platform scales.
Use for delete, payout release, refund, reject, suspend.
Toasts should include direct next action when useful.
